# Setting up two NUCs

## Basic Configuration: srsCU with two srsDU

For this setup, we will have the following deployment:

Machine 1: Core, Near RT RIC, srsRAN CU  
Machine 2: Two instances of srsDU

You need to first configure all of the IPs. Check the connection name with "nmcli device status" and see which connection is giving your machine its actual IP that is able to access the internet. Then, you can modify that connection using this command (must be edited depending on IP configuration). This is for the DU Machine:

'nmcli connection modify "Wired connection 3" \+ipv4.addresses "172.16.80.102/32"'

And same for 103 for each of the DU.   
Then I ran 'nmcli connection up "Wired connection 3"'

You then need to edit this file in each of the srsRAN directories (you need two different installs of srsRAN, but note that you CANNOT do 'sudo make install' when installing them, just 'sudo make' should be enough):  
\~/srsRAN\_Project/apps/helpers/f1u/f1u\_appconfig.h  
![][image1]  
Change the bind address in that file to match whatever IP you want to give to each DU, then do 'sudo make' in the base srsRAN directory to save the configuration. 

See all connections for that IP with 'nmcli connection show "Wired connection 3" | grep ipv4.addresses'  
For the configuration of RIC and Core on one machine and both DUs on another machine, we are configuring with these parameters for srsRAN commit 2be82d8ea38e3a729850b702254952c04118cc38:

In CU machine, we have configured there to be three IP addresses:

cciadmin@CCI-NUC2:\~/srsRAN\_Project$ nmcli connection show "enp112s0" | grep ipv4.addresses  
ipv4.addresses:                         172.16.80.2/32, 172.16.80.21/32, 172.16.80.22/32

For f1ap bind\_address we gave real machine IP which is the '.2' one. For f1u socket bind\_address we gave the new IP of '.21'

For the DU machine, we went into each folder mentioned prior and changed the specific value to some new IPs that we configured. The real machine IP is '.2' and we added these:

cciadmin@Non-RT-RIC:\~$ nmcli connection show "Wired connection 3" | grep ipv4.addresses  
ipv4.addresses:                         172.16.80.3/32, 172.16.80.102/32, 172.16.80.103/32, 172.16.80.12/32, 172.16.80.13/32

All of these were added using the nmcli command from earlier. Up to this point, we have not done E2 configuration. We will do that next.

For the DU configuration, we gave:

'172.16.80.2' for f1ap cu\_cp\_addr  
'172.16.80.102' for f1ap bind\_addr (should match hardcoded value)  
'172.16.80.12' for f1u socket

That should be all which is required for basic configuration. 

The Near RT RIC and CU are colocated, so for the E2 configuration we kept the address as 10.0.2.10 and bind address as 10.0.2.1 with port 36421, and these should be the defaults from srsRAN's version of the O-RAN SC RIC. 

For the DUs, we used the 172.16.80.21 and 172.16.80.22 IPs which were previously added to the CU machine using the nmcli command. Note that 172.16.80.21 was configured for the CU f1u socket bind\_address, but we are reusing it here for E2 as well. The DUs should be using the 172.16.80.21 or 172.16.80.22 IP as the RIC address while using their hardcoded F1AP addresses as the bind address. Both of them should be using port 36421, the same as what is configured in the CU. With that, configuration for srsCU with two srsDU is complete.

**CU Machine:**  
Terminal 1:  
/home/cciadmin/oran-sc-ric\# docker compose up

(You can fully turn it off with `docker compose down`)

Terminal 2:  
/home/cciadmin/oran-sc-ric\# docker compose exec python\_xapp\_runner ./ho\_v3.py

Terminal 3:  
/home/cciadmin/Github/open5gs/build/tests/app\# ./5gc

Terminal 4:  
/home/cciadmin/srsRAN\_Project/build/apps/cu\# ./srscu \-c b210\_cu\_handover.yml

Terminal 5:  
\~/metrics-dashboard$ npm start

**DU Machine:**  
Terminal 1:  
/home/cciadmin/srsRAN\_Project/build/apps/du\# ./srsdu \-c du1.yml

Terminal 2:  
/home/cciadmin/du2/srsRAN\_Project/build/apps/du\# ./srsdu \-c du2.yml 

On both phones:  
iperf3 \-s

For UE with SIM of 6767:  
iperf3 \-c 10.45.0.6 \-t 5000 \-i 1 \-b 10M \-u  
iperf3 \-c 10.45.0.6 \-t 5000 \-i 1 \-b 50M \-u  
iperf3 \-c 10.45.0.6 \-t 5000 \-i 1 \-b 10M \-u

For UE with SIM of 6789:  
iperf3 \-c 10.45.0.7 \-t 5000 \-i 1 \-b 20M \-u  
![][image2]  
![][image3]

## 

## 

## Interoperable Configuration: srsCU with srsDU and OAI DU

For this setup, we will have the following deployment:

Machine 1: Core, Near RT RIC, srsRAN CU  
Machine 2: One instance of srsDU and one instance of OAI DU (\*new)

This also assumes that the previous procedure has been followed.

# Interoperable Scenario

When going to install the OAI DU on the NUC which has both of the srsDUs on it, you need to run the build commands as follows:

\`\`\`  
cd cmake\_targets/  
./build\_oai \-I  
./build\_oai \-w USRP \-/-ninja \-/-gNB \-C \-/-noavx512  
\`\`\`

When going to install OAI CU on the NUC which has the Core and RIC, you need to run the build command like this (get rid of the slashes):  
\`\`\`  
cd cmake\_targets/  
./build\_oai \-I  
./build\_oai \-w USRP \-/-ninja \-/-gNB \-C  
\`\`\`

I don't know why the other machine forces the AVX512 which is a compilation optimization. We have to disable that in order for the compilation to be successful. 

\~/Github/open5gs/build/tests/app$ sudo ./5gc

\~/oai/cmake\_targets/ran\_build/build$  sudo ./nr-softmodem \-O \~/oai/targets/PROJECTS/GENERIC-NR-5GC/CONF/b210\_oai\_cu.conf \-E \--sa \--debug-phy

\~/srsRAN\_Project/build/apps/du$  sudo srsdu \-c b210\_srs\_du.yml

\~/f1\_development/openairinterface5g/cmake\_targets/ran\_build/build$ sudo /home/cciadmin/f1\_development/openairinterface5g/cmake\_targets/ran\_build/build/nr-softmodem \-O ../../../targets/PROJECTS/GENERIC-NR-5GC/CONF/matching\_gnb.conf \-E

# Two srsDU with one srsCU: 

\~/srsRAN\_Project/build/apps/cu$ sudo srscu \-c srscu\_mobile\_ric.yml 

\~/srsRAN\_Project/build/apps/du$ sudo srsdu \-c 1\_b210\_mobile\_ric\_du.yml  
\~/srsRAN\_Project/build/apps/du$ sudo srsdu \-c 2\_b210\_mobile\_ric\_du.yml 

run tunnel script  
/tmp$ tail \-f srscu\_mobile\_ric.log | grep handover

We changed this file:  
\~/srsRAN\_Project/apps/helpers/f1u/f1u\_appconfig.h

The bind address was updated to be the machine IP.

\~/Github/open5gs/build/tests/app$ sudo ./5gc

\~/srsRAN\_Project/build/apps/cu$ sudo srscu \-c test\_srs\_oai\_cu.yml

\~/srsRAN\_Project/build/apps/du$ sudo srsdu \-c 1\_test\_srs\_du.yml

\~/srsRAN\_Project/build/apps/du$ sudo srsdu \-c 2\_test\_srs\_du.yml

Findings:  
To run with two DUs, I need to set the sector\_id parameter in the DU to match the expected nr\_cell\_id parameter in the CU configuration. Also, I needed to create a new WiFi interface in order for both DUs to access the CU on the same port (2152) using this command: `sudo ip addr add 192.168.1.174/24 dev wlp4s0` (note that the xxx.xxx.x.174 is just the original WiFi IP with \+1 added to the last octet).

# One OAI DU with srsCU:

When going to install the OAI DU on the NUC which has both of the srsDUs on it, you need to run the build commands as follows:

\`\`\`  
cd cmake\_targets/  
./build\_oai \-I  
./build\_oai \-w USRP –ninja –gNB \-C –noavx512  
\`\`\`

\~/Github/open5gs/build/tests/app$ sudo ./5gc

\`\`\`  
timestamp=$(date \+%Y%m%d\_%H%M%S) && \\  
sudo ./5gc 2\>&1 | tee /tmp/open5gs\_5gc\_${timestamp}.log  
\`\`\`

\~/srsRAN\_Project/build/apps/cu$ sudo ./srscu \-c iot\_1\_oai\_du.yml

\~/openairinterface5g/cmake\_targets/ran\_build/build$ sudo ./nr-softmodem \-O \~/openairinterface5g/targets/PROJECTS/GENERIC-NR-5GC/CONF/oai\_du\_srs\_cu\_du1.conf \-E \--sa \--debug-phy

\`\`\`  
timestamp=$(date \+%Y%m%d\_%H%M%S) && \\  
sudo ./nr-softmodem \-O \~/oai/targets/PROJECTS/GENERIC-NR-5GC/CONF/oai\_du\_srs\_cu\_du1.conf \-E \--sa \--debug-phy \\  
2\>&1 | tee /tmp/oai\_du\_all\_${timestamp}.log  
\`\`\`

Findings:  
I made the OAI configuration so that the B210 had maximum attenuation and also removed the antennas entirely. Then, I ran srsDU with normal configuration. I found that the UE attached fine to the srsDU and had internet access. I also tested with starting from the OAI DU and got same issues as before:   
![][image4]  
Though, the Core was not showing those tunnel errors this time…

Next I will test the CU handover command from one srsDU to another srsDU:

Start with UE attached to DU 1 (PCI 1):  
![][image5]

Start DU 2 (PCI 2):  
![][image6]

Run HO command in CU:  
![][image7]

It works\! (have not confirmed via logs)  
![][image8]

UE stopped printing the info on DU 1 and it gave some output in DU 2\.

Now I will test the srsCU HO command with OAI DU:

![][image9]

UE attempts to HO to DU 2 (OAI) but fails. RRC failed to establish a connection with the target cell, and then performed an RRC re-establishment procedure with the source cell, receiving a new RNTI (4602) in the process.

OAI output:  
![][image10]

Once again, the issue comes with OAI's deployment. If they ask, getting the logs for this will not be a problem. Wait for Robert to return to share this with him.

# One srsDU with OAI CU:

Slice level PRB Quota:  
8.4.3.6

8.4.3.6	

Slice-level PRB quota 

Upon receiving the *RIC Control Request* message, the E2 node allocates slice-specific PRB quota for the indicated S-NSSAI in terms of the maximum PRB allocation ratio, minimum PRB allocation ratio and dedicated PRB allocation ratio for the given slice among the available set of PRBs. If the *S-NSSAI* is missing in the *RIC Control Request* message, the E2 node will send a *RIC Control Failure*. 

| RAN Parameter ID  | RAN Parameter  | RAN Parameter Value Type  | Key Flag  | RAN Parameter Definition  | *Semantics Description*  |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | :---: | ----- | ----- |
| **1**  | RRM Policy Ratio List  | LIST  |   | *0.. \<maxnoofRRMPolicyRatioGroups\>*  | RRMPolicyRatioList in TS 28.541  |
| **2**  | \>RRM Policy Ratio Group  | STRUCTURE  |   |   | RRMPolicyRatio in TS 28.541  |
| 3  | \>\>RRM Policy  | STRUCTURE  |   |   | RRMPolicy in TS 28.541  |
| 5  | \>\>RRM Policy Member List  | LIST  |   | *0..\<maxnoofRRMPolicyMembers\>*  | *RRMPolicyMemberList* in TS 28.541  |
| 6  | \>\>\>\>RRM Policy Member  | STRUCTURE  |   |   | *RRMPolicyMember* in TS 28.541  |
| 7  | \>\>\>\>\>PLMN Identity  | ELEMENT  | FALSE  | *PLMN Identity* IE in TS 38.473 \[19\] Clause 9.3.1.14  |   |
| 8  | \>\>\>\>\>S-NSSAI  | STRUCTURE  |   |   | *S-NSSAI* IE in TS 38.473 \[19\] Clause 9.3.1.38  |
| 9  | \>\>\>\>\>\>SST  | ELEMENT  | FALSE  | *SST* IE in TS 38.473 \[19\] Clause 9.3.1.38  |   |
| 10  | \>\>\>\>\>\>SD  | ELEMENT  | FALSE  | *SD* IE in TS 38.473 \[19\] Clause 9.3.1.38  |   |
| 11  | \>\>Min PRB Policy Ratio  | ELEMENT  | FALSE  | INTEGER (0..100)  | rRMPolicyMinRatio IE in TS 28.541  |
| 12  | \>\>Max PRB Policy Ratio  | ELEMENT  | FALSE  | INTEGER (0..100)  | rRMPolicyMaxRatio IE in TS 28.541  |
| 13  | \>\>Dedicated PRB Policy Ratio  | ELEMENT  | FALSE  | INTEGER (0..100)  | rRMPolicyDedicatedRatio IE in TS 28.541  |

 

| Range bound  | Explanation  |
| :---- | ----- |
| maxnoofRRMPolicyRatioGroups  | Maximum no. of RAN parameters supported by RAN Function for a specific Control action. The value is \<65535\>.  |
| maxnoofRRMPolicyMembers  | Maximum no. of RAN parameters supported by RAN Function for a specific Control action. The value is \<65535\>  |

 

8.4.4.1	

Handover Control 

Upon receiving the *RIC Control Request* message, in the case of Xn/X2 or NG or inter-RAT handovers, the E2 node shall invoke procedures, such as *Handover Preparation* related to UE Mobility Management, *Bearer Context Modification, UE Context Modification*, *RRC Message Transfer*, etc. In the case of intra-gNB or F1 handover, the E2 node shall invoke procedures, such as *UE Context Modification*, *RRC Message Transfer*, etc. The E2 node includes the IEs corresponding to one or more of parameters described below in the related interface messages. If the *Target Primary Cell ID* IE is missing in the *RIC Control Request* message, the E2 node will send a *RIC Control Failure* message. 

| RAN Parameter ID  | RAN Parameter  | RAN Parameter Value Type  | Key Flag  | RAN Parameter Definition  | Semantics Description  |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | :---: | ----- | ----- |
| 1  | Target Primary Cell ID  | STRUCTURE  |   |   | *Target Cell Global ID* IE in TS 38.423 \[15\] Clause 9.2.3.25  |
| 2  | \>CHOICE Target Cell  | STRUCTURE  |   |   | *Target Cell* IE in TS 38.423 \[15\] Clause 9.2.3.25  |
| 3  | \>\>NR Cell  | STRUCTURE  |   |   | *NR* IE in TS 38.423 \[15\] Clause 9.2.3.25  |
| 4  | \>\>\>NR CGI  | ELEMENT  | FALSE  | *NR CGI* IE in TS 38.423 \[15\] Clause 9.2.2.7  |   |
| 5  | \>\>E-UTRA Cell  | STRUCTURE  |   |   | *E-UTRA* IE in TS 38.423 \[15\] Clause 9.2.3.25  |
| 6  | \>\>\>E-UTRA CGI  | ELEMENT  | FALSE  | *E-UTRA CGI* IE in TS 38.423 \[15\] Clause 9.2.2.8  |   |
| 7  | List of PDU sessions for handover  | LIST  |   |   | *PDU Session Resources To Be Setup List* IE in TS 38.423 \[15\] Clause 9.2.1.1  |
| 8  | \>PDU session Item for handover  | STRUCTURE  |   |   | *PDU Session Resources To Be Setup Item* IE in TS 38.423 \[15\] Clause 9.2.1.1  |
| 9  | \>\>PDU Session ID  | ELEMENT  | TRUE  | *PDU Session ID* IE in TS 38.423 \[15\] Clause 9.2.3.18  |   |
| 10  | \>\>List of QoS flows in the PDU session  | LIST  |   |   | *QoS Flows To Be Setup List* IE in TS 38.423 \[15\] Clause 9.2.1.1  |
| 11  | \>\>\>QoS flow Item  | STRUCTURE  |   |   | *QoS Flow To Be Setup Item* IE in TS 38.423 \[15\] Clause 9.2.1.1  |
| 12  | \>\>\>\>QoS Flow Identifier  | ELEMENT  | TRUE  | *QoS Flow Identifier* IE in TS 38.423 \[15\] Clause 9.2.3.10  |   |
| 13  | List of DRBs for handover  | LIST  |   |   | *DRB to Be Setup List* IE in TS 38.473 \[19\] Clause 9.2.2.1  |
| 14  | \>DRB item for handover  | STRUCTURE  |   |   | *DRB to Be Setup Item* IE in TS 38.473 \[19\] Clause 9.2.2.1  |
| 15  | \>\>DRB ID  | ELEMENT  | TRUE  | *DRB ID* IE in TS 38.473 \[19\] Clause 9.3.1.8  |   |
| 16  | \>\>List of QoS flows in the DRB  | LIST  |   |   | *QoS Flows Information To Be Setup* IE in TS 37.483 \[21\] Clause 9.3.3.2  |
| 17  | \>\>\>QoS flow Item  | STRUCTURE  |   |   | *QoS Flow Item* IE in TS 37.483 \[21\] Clause 9.3.1.25  |
| 18  | \>\>\>\>QoS flow Identifier  | ELEMENT  | TRUE  | *QoS Flow Identifier* IE in TS 37.483 \[21\] Clause 9.3.1.24  |   |
| 19  | List of Secondary cells to be setup  | LIST  |   |   | *Scell To Be Setup List* IE in TS 38.473 \[19\] Clause 9.2.2.1  |
| 20  | \>Secondary cell Item to be setup  | STRUCTURE  |   |   | *Scell To Be Setup Item Ies* IE in TS 38.473 \[19\] Clause 9.2.2.1  |
| 21  | \>\>Secondary cell ID  | ELEMENT  | FALSE  | *Scell ID* IE in TS 38.473 \[19\] Clause 9.2.2.1  |   |

 \+-----+----------------------------------------+---------------------------+----------+----------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------+

| RAN | RAN Parameter                          | RAN Parameter Value Type  | Key Flag | RAN Parameter Definition               | Semantics Description                                              |

| Par |                                        |                           |          |                                        |                                                                    |

| ID  |                                        |                           |          |                                        |                                                                    |

\+-----+----------------------------------------+---------------------------+----------+----------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------+

| 1   | Target Primary Cell ID                 | STRUCTURE                 |          |                                        | Target Cell Global ID IE in TS 38.423 Clause 9.2.3.25              |

| 2   | \>CHOICE Target Cell                    | STRUCTURE                 |          |                                        | Target Cell IE in TS 38.423 Clause 9.2.3.25                        |

| 3   | \>\>NR Cell                              | STRUCTURE                 |          |                                        | NR IE in TS 38.423 Clause 9.2.3.25                                 |

| 4   | \>\>\>NR CGI                              | ELEMENT                   | FALSE    | NR CGI IE in TS 38.423 Clause 9.2.2.7  |                                                                    |

| 5   | \>\>E-UTRA Cell                          | STRUCTURE                 |          |                                        | E-UTRA IE in TS 38.423 Clause 9.2.3.25                             |

| 6   | \>\>\>E-UTRA CGI                          | ELEMENT                   | FALSE    | E-UTRA CGI IE in TS 38.423 Clause 9.2.2.8 |                                                                  |

| 7   | List of PDU sessions for handover      | LIST                      |          |                                        | PDU Session Resources To Be Setup List IE in TS 38.423 Clause 9.2.1.1 |

| 8   | \>PDU session Item for handover         | STRUCTURE                 |          |                                        | PDU Session Resources To Be Setup Item IE in TS 38.423 Clause 9.2.1.1 |

| 9   | \>\>PDU Session ID                       | ELEMENT                   | TRUE     | PDU Session ID IE in TS 38.423 Clause 9.2.3.18 |                                                                |

| 10  | \>\>List of QoS flows in the PDU session | LIST                      |          |                                        | QoS Flows To Be Setup List IE in TS 38.423 Clause 9.2.1.1           |

| 11  | \>\>\>QoS flow Item                       | STRUCTURE                 |          |                                        | QoS Flow To Be Setup Item IE in TS 38.423 Clause 9.2.1.1            |

| 12  | \>\>\>\>QoS Flow Identifier                | ELEMENT                   | TRUE     | QoS Flow Identifier IE in TS 38.423 Clause 9.2.3.10 |                                                        |

| 13  | List of DRBs for handover              | LIST                      |          |                                        | DRB to Be Setup List IE in TS 38.473 Clause 9.2.2.1                |

| 14  | \>DRB item for handover                 | STRUCTURE                 |          |                                        | DRB to Be Setup Item IE in TS 38.473 Clause 9.2.2.1                |

| 15  | \>\>DRB ID                               | ELEMENT                   | TRUE     | DRB ID IE in TS 38.473 Clause 9.3.1.8   |                                                                   |

| 16  | \>\>List of QoS flows in the DRB         | LIST                      |          |                                        | QoS Flows Information To Be Setup IE in TS 37.483 Clause 9.3.3.2   |

| 17  | \>\>\>QoS flow Item                       | STRUCTURE                 |          |                                        | QoS Flow Item IE in TS 37.483 Clause 9.3.1.25                      |

| 18  | \>\>\>\>QoS flow Identifier                | ELEMENT                   | TRUE     | QoS Flow Identifier IE in TS 37.483 Clause 9.3.1.24  |                                                 |

| 19  | List of Secondary cells to be setup    | LIST                      |          |                                        | Scell To Be Setup List IE in TS 38.473 Clause 9.2.2.1              |

| 20  | \>Secondary cell Item to be setup       | STRUCTURE                 |          |                                        | Scell To Be Setup Item Ies IE in TS 38.473 Clause 9.2.2.1          |

| 21  | \>\>Secondary cell ID                    | ELEMENT                   | FALSE    | Scell ID IE in TS 38.473 Clause 9.2.2.1 |                                                                  |

\+-----+----------------------------------------+---------------------------+----------+----------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------+

# Testing srsCU with RIC:

cciadmin@CCI-NUC2:\~/srsRAN\_Project/build/apps/cu$ sudo srscu \-c ric\_cu\_test.yml

# CU E2 Executor Code:

[srsRAN\_Project/lib/e2/e2sm/e2sm\_rc/e2sm\_rc\_control\_action\_cu\_executor.cpp at 8120ffbce1f802542c9256fd2104f45c86b7e488 · srsran/srsRAN\_Project](https://github.com/srsran/srsRAN_Project/blob/8120ffbce1f802542c9256fd2104f45c86b7e488/lib/e2/e2sm/e2sm_rc/e2sm_rc_control_action_cu_executor.cpp)

Let's examine the key part of the executor code:

async\_task\<e2sm\_ric\_control\_response\>  
e2sm\_rc\_control\_action\_3\_1\_cu\_executor::execute\_ric\_control\_action(const e2sm\_ric\_control\_request& req)  
{  
  return launch\_async(\[this, req\](coro\_context\<async\_task\<e2sm\_ric\_control\_response\>\>& ctx) {  
    srs\_cu\_cp::cu\_cp\_intra\_cu\_handover\_response cu\_cp\_response;  
    srs\_cu\_cp::cu\_cp\_intra\_cu\_handover\_request  handover\_req;  
    CORO\_BEGIN(ctx);  
    CORO\_AWAIT\_VALUE(cu\_cp\_response,  
                     cu\_param\_configurator.get\_mobility\_notifier().on\_intra\_cu\_handover\_required(  
                         handover\_req, srs\_cu\_cp::du\_index\_t(0), srs\_cu\_cp::du\_index\_t(1)));  
    e2sm\_ric\_control\_response e2sm\_response;  
    e2sm\_response.success \= cu\_cp\_response.success;  
    CORO\_RETURN(e2sm\_response);  
  });  
}

When the executor receives our E2 message, it will:

1. Create an empty `handover_req` object of type `srs_cu_cp::cu_cp_intra_cu_handover_request`  
2. Call `on_intra_cu_handover_required` with this empty request and hardcoded DU indices (0 and 1\)  
3. Wait for the response  
4. Return an E2SM control response with success status based on the handover result

The crucial observation is that **the executor doesn't actually parse any parameters from our E2 message**. This is confirmed by the empty implementation of the parameter parsing method:

void e2sm\_rc\_control\_action\_3\_1\_cu\_executor::parse\_action\_ran\_parameter\_value(  
    const asn1::e2sm::ran\_param\_value\_type\_c& ran\_p,  
    uint64\_t                                  ran\_param\_id,  
    uint64\_t                                  ue\_id,  
    cu\_handover\_control\_config&               ctrl\_cfg)  
{  
}

So, this CU executor will:

1. Attempt to perform a handover when it receives our message ✅  
2. Always use hardcoded DU indices (0 → 1), ignoring our custom parameters ⚠️  
3. Not use any of the target cell information we provided ⚠️  
4. Return a success/failure response ✅

In other words, the executor will initiate a handover, but it won't use any of the parameters from our carefully constructed message. It will simply trigger a fixed handover operation from DU index 0 to DU index 1\.

For a real-world implementation, the executor would need to be enhanced to properly parse the Target Primary Cell ID and other parameters from the E2 control message, and use that information to populate the `handover_req` structure.

The `handover_req` object is created but never populated with any data \- including any UE identifier. The UE ID from the E2 control message (in the control header) is never extracted or used.

When there's only one UE in the network:

* It might work by coincidence, since there's only one UE for the system to handover  
* The mobility management might use default values or make assumptions when no specific UE is identified  
* But this would be implementation-dependent behavior, not something guaranteed by the code we can see

This implementation has a significant limitation:

1. The executor isn't properly using the UE ID from the control message  
2. It doesn't specify which UE should be handed over in the `handover_req`  
3. The empty `parse_action_ran_parameter_value()` method confirms it's not processing the message parameters

For a proper implementation, the executor should:

1. Extract the UE ID from the control message header  
2. Use that UE ID to populate the handover request  
3. Parse the target cell information from the message parameters

Without these improvements, the current implementation is incomplete and would only work reliably in a single-UE test environment or under specific conditions where the system can determine which UE to handover by other means.

Follow HO command path:

This command uses serving PCI, neighbor PCI, UE RNTI  
[https://github.com/srsran/srsRAN\_Project/blob/2be82d8ea38e3a729850b702254952c04118cc38/apps/units/o\_cu\_cp/cu\_cp/cu\_cp\_cmdline\_commands.h\#L78](https://github.com/srsran/srsRAN_Project/blob/2be82d8ea38e3a729850b702254952c04118cc38/apps/units/o_cu_cp/cu_cp/cu_cp_cmdline_commands.h#L78)

We want to implement:

void mobility\_manager::handle\_intra\_cu\_handover(ue\_index\_t source\_ue\_index,  
                                                pci\_t      neighbor\_pci,  
                                                du\_index\_t source\_du\_index,  
                                                du\_index\_t target\_du\_index)  
[https://github.com/srsran/srsRAN\_Project/blob/2be82d8ea38e3a729850b702254952c04118cc38/lib/cu\_cp/mobility\_manager/mobility\_manager\_impl.cpp\#L114](https://github.com/srsran/srsRAN_Project/blob/2be82d8ea38e3a729850b702254952c04118cc38/lib/cu_cp/mobility_manager/mobility_manager_impl.cpp#L114)

[https://github.com/srsran/srsRAN\_Project/blob/2be82d8ea38e3a729850b702254952c04118cc38/lib/e2/e2sm/e2sm\_rc/e2sm\_rc\_control\_action\_cu\_executor.cpp\#L88](https://github.com/srsran/srsRAN_Project/blob/2be82d8ea38e3a729850b702254952c04118cc38/lib/e2/e2sm/e2sm_rc/e2sm_rc_control_action_cu_executor.cpp#L88)

[https://github.com/srsran/srsRAN\_Project/blob/2be82d8ea38e3a729850b702254952c04118cc38/lib/cu\_cp/cu\_cp\_impl.cpp\#L734](https://github.com/srsran/srsRAN_Project/blob/2be82d8ea38e3a729850b702254952c04118cc38/lib/cu_cp/cu_cp_impl.cpp#L734)

There is a function which is using same 

[Resolving srsRAN Handover Error \- Claude](https://claude.ai/chat/b48baca0-f4aa-44ca-9e2a-5e2b10315935)  
[Validating Handover Implementation Approach \- Claude](https://claude.ai/chat/6d28b881-3687-45fa-a1d7-7e071117b749)  
[Detailed Guide to Fix CU Executor for xApp \- Claude](https://claude.ai/chat/c9de918a-c67e-492a-863b-e670e52b35dd)

# New Testing

## srsRAN CU with srsRAN DU: ![][image11]

Between this configuration and the one with OAI DU, we do not see that the F1-U is *receiving* any SDUs. But in both configurations, F1-U is delivering T-PDUs. 

![][image12]  
A PDU Session is created and IP is allocated. UE has internet access in this configuration.

## srsRAN CU with OAI DU: ![][image13]

![][image14]

PDU Session is created and IP is allocated to UE but there is no internet access.

When srsCU+srsDU is run, then srsCU+OAI DU is run, the UE attach behaves as usual. However, when the core is not shutdown throughout this process, and we run srsCU+srsDU again, we find that the DU is creating phantom UEs, and the Core registers the same UE twice:  
![][image15]  
![][image16]

Restarting the Core and then starting srsCU with srsDU results in normal logs:  
![][image17]

From a basic observation of the srsCU \+ OAI DU combination and srsCU \+ OAI DU Core logs, we see no significant difference between the IP addresses of the gtp\_connect() and various other endpoint connection setups. 

## OAI CU with OAI DU:

![][image18]

Not sure whether this error impacts anything. 

![][image19]  
![][image20]

When we enable flight mode on the UE, we see in the OAI CU logs that the GTPU function is unable to retrieve the UE ID from the ue\_mapping hashtable, resulting in the red error message. Then, the core prints this message:  
![][image21]

Also, in the OAI DU logs, after we put the UE on flight mode, it continued to attempt to send packets:  
![][image22]  
![][image23]  
After some time, the OAI DU releases the UE with no user input:  
![][image24]

## OAI CU with srsRAN DU:

![][image25]

This configuration gives UE a PDU session and IP/internet access. 

The issues are similar to the OAI CU \+ OAI DU combination, the UE Release procedure is not working properly. 

When we set the UE to flight mode, the DU maintains the UE connection:  
![][image26]  
Then, when we reattach the UE, the phantom UE disappears and only the real UE connection remains, but a similar pattern is observed after we set that UE on flight mode as well:  
![][image27]  
The Core also shows similar problems with the UE release:  
![][image28]
